commands_analyzed.name: intelligent-refactor-session.md
commands_analyzed.reality_check: 534-line monster violating its own simplicity principles

savage_analysis.evidence: Claims intelligence while using basic if/then logic
savage_analysis.recommendation: Delete 80% of the content, keep the core functionality

commands_analyzed.name: adaptive-complexity-router.md
commands_analyzed.promises: Intelligent task routing based on automatic complexity scoring
commands_analyzed.reality_check: Software astrology with arbitrary point assignments

savage_analysis.evidence: Lines 65-108 contain scoring system designed by committee of confused interns
savage_analysis.contradiction: Claims adaptation while being rigid XML templates

commands_analyzed.name: java-rapid-implementation.md
commands_analyzed.promises: Quick Java implementation with simplicity focus
commands_analyzed.reality_check: 250 lines explaining how to write simple Java - defeats its own purpose

savage_analysis.evidence: Takes longer to read the 'rapid' instructions than to just write the Java
savage_analysis.contradiction: Promotes simplicity while adding complexity layers
savage_analysis.recommendation: Replace with: 'Write simple Java. Use existing libraries. Done.'

commands_analyzed.name: analyze-project-architecture.md
commands_analyzed.promises: Comprehensive codebase analysis for architecture patterns
commands_analyzed.reality_check: Has potential but drowns in pseudo-philosophical thinking theater

savage_analysis.evidence: Actually useful core functionality buried under philosophical debris
savage_analysis.contradiction: Promises comprehensive analysis but spends more time explaining thinking than thinking
savage_analysis.recommendation: Extract the 30% that's valuable, burn the rest

commands_analyzed.name: adhd-morning-assistant.md
commands_analyzed.promises: AI-powered morning planning assistant that learns from patterns
commands_analyzed.reality_check: Only command that understands its user, but still over-engineered

savage_analysis.evidence: Actually solves real problems for real users with specific needs
savage_analysis.contradiction: Could be 50% shorter without losing functionality
savage_analysis.recommendation: Keep core features, trim the presentation layer

patterns_identified: Thinking Theater Syndrome: Excessive meta-cognitive documentation
Complexity Projection: Commands violating their own simplicity rules
Analysis Paralysis Codification: Over-engineering simple decisions
Philosophical Pollution: Abstract concepts drowning concrete actions

immediate_actions: Trim 'adhd-morning-assistant.md' - reduce by 40% while keeping core
systemic_fixes: Implement 'Thinking Token Tax': Every <thinking> block costs 2x tokens
Enforce 'One Screen Rule': Commands must fit on single screen
Apply 'Grandmother Test': If your grandmother can't understand it, rewrite it
Institute 'Value Density Minimum': 50% of content must be actionable
quality_gates: Lines of philosophy / lines of action < 1.0
User can execute within 30 seconds of reading

methodology_notes.measurement_approach: Token counting, line analysis, contradiction detection, utility assessment
methodology_notes.bias_acknowledgment: Deliberately harsh to counter selection bias and over-engineering tendencies
methodology_notes.confidence_intervals: 95% confidence in measurements, 90% confidence in recommendations

conclusion.primary_pathology: Chronic Over-Engineering with Thinking Theater Syndrome
conclusion.prognosis: Recovery possible with aggressive simplification therapy
